problem_summary: 2024 MCM Problem B ("Searching for Submersibles"): build models to (1) predict over time the location of a tourist submersible that has lost propulsion/communication in the Ionian Sea, accounting for currents, seawater-density variation, and seafloor geography; (2) recommend search/communication equipment for the host ship and rescue vessels; (3) define initial search deployment points and a search pattern that minimizes time-to-find, expressing probability-of-finding as a function of time and accumulated results; and (4) extrapolate to other seas and to multiple co-located submersibles. This Outstanding paper (Team 2425792) frames it as a Localization -> Uncertainty-Reduction -> Search-and-Rescue pipeline driven by a physics-based motion model fused with a Kalman filter.
models_used: Fossen 6-DOF rigid-body marine-craft dynamics model (M*v_dot + C(v)v + D(v)v + g(eta) = tau + F_prop), simplified to a constant-depth translational CFD form m*dv/dt = F_current + F_prop with F_prop=0 after malfunction
Hydrodynamic drag/current force F_current = -0.5*rho*Cd*A*||v_sub||*v_sub using relative velocity v_sub = v - u_current; propeller thrust ||F_prop|| = (1-t)*rho*n^2*d^2*K_T

Linear Kalman Filter for 3D state estimation: 9-dim state [position, velocity, acceleration], constant-acceleration block state-transition matrix F (I3 / dt*I3 / 0.5dt^2*I3 layout), observation vector fusing IMU velocity+acceleration and CFD-derived total force, observation matrix H with I3/m block; control input deliberately zeroed (B=0,u=0)
Multivariate (3D) Gaussian distribution over position built from the Kalman predicted error covariance P_{k|k-1}, used to derive a 95% confidence ellipsoid as the 'feasible search zone'

Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on a 14-device x 13-indicator standardized matrix; eigendecomposition of covariance, 3 retained PCs (cumulative contribution 85.66%) interpreted as cost-effectiveness / operational reliability / human-resource, composite score = contribution-weighted PC scores to rank equipment

Sonar-coverage geometry model: conical 3-degree acoustic beam projecting an ellipse on a sloped seabed, yielding the 'Along-Isobath Search' (move perpendicular to the depth gradient to maximize swath width per unit time)

Simple decay probability-of-detection model P(found|t) = 1/(alpha*t), alpha proportional to seabed-environment entropy; deliberately memoryless (Occam's razor justification for ignoring past-search history)

Particle-dispersion extension: multiple hypothetical particles each tracked by Kalman filters, low-probability particles pruned and regenerated in high-probability zones (informal particle-filter flavor)
Voronoi diagram tessellation for multi-submersible cooperation/collision-avoidance and failure-response zoning
key_techniques: Data fusion: combining onboard sensor streams (IMU velocity/acceleration, environmental sensors) with the CFD-predicted dynamics inside the KF measurement update, jointly reducing prediction error

Deliberate KF design choices justified physically: zeroing the control input B/u so noisy uncertain inputs don't corrupt the state; tuning Q and R by extensive empirical testing (explicit Q,R block matrices given) to trust high-precision sensors over CFD output

Graceful degradation: on communication loss they prune the sensor-based rows from the observation matrix so the filter keeps running on the CFD prediction alone — directly answers the 'lost communication' branch of the problem
Reuse of one core artifact across tasks: the same KF covariance P feeds both point localization (Task 1) and the 95% confidence feasible zone (Task 3), giving the paper strong internal coherence
Geometric optimization argument (isobath = max swath) instead of a heavy optimizer — a clean, intuitive, defensible result

Sensitivity analysis via cosine similarity of predicted vs actual trajectory plus RMSE under perturbation of rho, F_current, n, m (1000 iterations, dt=0.1s), ranking sensitivities High/Medium/Low with concrete RMSE values (1.55m, 2.10m, 1.12m, 1.17m)

Honest negative result: argues with Occam's razor that using past-search history is counterproductive (submersible drifts; risk of overfitting), so they choose a deterministic search trajectory over a probabilistic one

Strong problem framing devices: Titan-incident hook, two-type defect taxonomy (lost comms vs lost propulsion) with the simplifying assumption they co-occur, and a Localization->Reduction->Search narrative spine
why_it_won: "It pairs a credible, named physics backbone (Fossen 6-DOF / CFD) with the textbook-correct statistical tool for noisy recursive estimation (Kalman filter), and — crucially — reuses the filter's covariance to answer multiple sub-tasks (point location, 95% feasible zone, particle dispersion). That integration makes the four tasks read as one coherent system rather than four disconnected mini-models, which judges reward heavily. The KF design shows genuine engineering judgment (zeroed control input, pruning observation rows on comms loss, hand-tuned Q/R) rather than black-box application. It directly and explicitly addresses every bullet of the prompt (equipment via PCA, deployment point = last signal, search-time minimization via isobath geometry, probability-vs-time function, Caribbean extrapolation, multi-submersible via Voronoi), and backs claims with MATLAB simulations, a quantified sensitivity/error table, and a polished government memo. The writing is confident, well-structured, and uses vivid framing (Titan, defect taxonomy) — it tells a complete, decision-ready story."
section_highlights: "Summary uses a memorable four-verb spine — 'to locate / to prepare / to search / to extrapolate' — that previews the whole pipeline in one page. Assumptions are each paired with an explicit Justification (spherical isotropy, neutral buoyancy, invariant density, simultaneous defect), pre-empting judge objections. Task 1 derives the KF cleanly: state/observation vectors, block-form F and H matrices, and the explicit Q/R tuning matrices — high mathematical legibility. Task 2's PCA reports the actual eigenvector loadings, per-PC and cumulative contribution rates (47.79/70.46/85.66%), and a full composite-score ranking table of all 14 named real-world devices. Task 3 elegantly repurposes P_{k|k-1} into a 95% confidence ellipsoid and gives the isobath-swath geometric argument with a sonar-cone-ellipse figure. Section 7 is a rigorous sensitivity/error table (cosine similarity + RMSE, High/Med/Low). Strengths/Weaknesses section is candid (admits chaotic-trajectory pruning is ad hoc and that a probabilistic search could beat the deterministic one). Closes with a genuinely formatted memo to named Greek tourism officials."
pitfalls_or_limitations: The probability-of-finding model P(found|t)=1/(alpha*t) is hand-waved: alpha is 'proportional to entropy of the seabed' but never quantified, the form isn't derived or fit, and it is unbounded/ill-behaved (P>1 as t->0). This is the weakest answer to an explicit prompt requirement (probability as a function of time AND cumulative search results) — and they essentially opt out of the cumulative-results part

Choosing a deterministic isobath search over a probabilistic/Bayesian search is defended by Occam's razor, but the canonical MSAR approach (Bayesian search theory / probability map updating) is exactly what the problem invites; this is a real modeling concession dressed up as a virtue

The 'particle dispersion' / prune-and-regenerate scheme is described narratively but never formalized — it gestures at a particle filter without the resampling math, so its results figures are not reproducible from the text

Heavy linearization/simplification of Fossen: heave, roll, pitch dropped and constant-depth assumed, yet the headline trajectory is a downward-diverging 3D spiral — some tension between the 2D-ish dynamics and the 3D result; current field u_current is treated as a given/idealized vortex rather than data-driven

Equipment naming is internally inconsistent (Summary/text say 'Perry Slingsby Multi-Beam, Side Scan Sonar, Divex AUV-1000' as the picks, but the composite-score table actually ranks Teledyne BlueView, Edgetech, and SMD Quantum EV highest — a traceability error a sharp judge would catch)

Q and R tuned purely by 'empirical testing' with no ground-truth validation dataset; RMSE values are vs a simulated 'actual' path the team also generated, so error metrics are self-referential, not externally validated
Caribbean extrapolation is largely qualitative (descriptions of currents/bathymetry) with no re-run numbers; Voronoi cooperation is conceptual with no dynamics or collision metrics
reusable_patterns: Localization -> Uncertainty-Reduction -> Search/Decision as a three-stage spine for any 'find a lost/moving object under noise' problem; pick a named physics model for stage 1 and a Kalman/Bayes filter for stage 2
Reuse a single statistical artifact (the KF error-covariance P) across multiple tasks — point estimate, confidence region, and search zone — to make a multi-part problem cohere
Convert an estimator's covariance into a decision region via a multivariate-Gaussian X% confidence ellipsoid; then layer an operational strategy (search/coverage) on top of that region

PCA-for-equipment-selection template: rows=candidate products, cols=performance indicators, standardize -> covariance -> eigendecompose -> keep PCs to ~85% cumulative -> interpret each PC in plain English -> contribution-weighted composite score ranking table

Sensitivity analysis combining a direction metric (cosine similarity of trajectories) with a magnitude metric (RMSE) under one-at-a-time parameter perturbation, summarized as a High/Med/Low + numeric table

Graceful-degradation modeling: explicitly describe how the model behaves when an input/sensor disappears (prune observation rows) — directly scores points when the prompt mentions failure/loss-of-signal
Geometric coverage argument (beam-cone -> ellipse -> move perpendicular to gradient for max swath) as a lightweight alternative to a full route optimizer

Framing scaffolding: open with a real-world incident hook (Titan), build a defect/failure taxonomy with a simplifying co-occurrence assumption, give each assumption a justification line, and close with a stakeholder memo in proper letter format

Use Occam's razor explicitly to justify excluding a tempting-but-fragile complication — and pair it with a candid Weaknesses section admitting the alternative might be better (signals modeling maturity to judges)
